name: Streamlit
what_it_does: Python-only framework for building interactive data apps and ML demos with minimal code. Write a Python script with Streamlit components (st.slider, st.dataframe, st.chart) and Streamlit renders a full web app — no HTML, CSS, or JavaScript needed. Acquired by Snowflake in 2022. Powers thousands of public ML demos on Hugging Face Spaces. Agent use: rapid UI wrappers around agent outputs, interactive data exploration dashboards.
use_cases: Build interactive data dashboards and ML model demos in pure Python without frontend skills — deploy agent output visualizations in hours
Create agent control panels with sliders, inputs, and real-time output streaming using st.write_stream for agent response display
Wrap agent pipelines with a shareable web UI for stakeholder demos without a separate frontend deployment
Build data exploration tools for agent-generated datasets with filtering, sorting, and charting in a single Python file
Deploy on Streamlit Community Cloud for free public sharing of agent demos and internal tools
not_for: Production web applications requiring custom layouts, routing, or complex state — use Reflex, FastAPI+HTMX, or React for production UIs
High-traffic applications — Streamlit runs Python per user session; each concurrent user spawns a Python process
Real-time streaming data at scale — Streamlit's session model is not suited for WebSocket-based live data pipelines
best_when: You need to quickly build an interactive Python data app or ML demo for internal use or prototyping, and don't want to write any frontend code.
avoid_when: You need multi-page routing, custom auth, high concurrency, or production-grade frontend features — use a proper web framework instead.

auth.notes: No auth built-in for the open-source framework — apps are publicly accessible by default. Streamlit Community Cloud adds Google OAuth for deployed apps. Self-hosted deployments require external auth (nginx, Cloudflare Access). Streamlit in Snowflake uses Snowflake RBAC.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: unlimited local use. Community Cloud: free hosting with limited resources and public apps.
pricing.paid_tiers: Streamlit in Snowflake via Snowflake compute credits; Community Cloud Teams for private apps
pricing.notes: Framework itself is Apache 2.0 open source. Streamlit Community Cloud offers free hosting for public apps. Private apps and enterprise features require Snowflake subscription or paid Community Cloud tier.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits on framework. Community Cloud limits: 1GB RAM, 1 CPU per app on free tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python tracebacks displayed directly in the app UI. st.exception() for custom error display. Script re-runs on error with full stack trace visible to developer.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Streamlit apps re-run entire Python script on each interaction — stateless by default. st.session_state for persistent values across reruns. Each user session is isolated.
known_agent_gotchas: Streamlit re-runs the entire script on every widget interaction — expensive agent calls must be cached with @st.cache_data or @st.cache_resource to avoid repeated execution
st.write_stream() enables streaming LLM output but requires generator functions — incompatible with some agent SDKs that use async generators
Session state (st.session_state) is per-browser-tab — multiple tabs for same user create separate isolated sessions with no shared state
Streamlit's threading model is not async — long-running agent calls block the UI thread; use st.spinner() and thread-based concurrency with care
Community Cloud apps sleep after inactivity (15 minutes free tier) — first user after sleep waits 30-60 seconds for cold start
Widget keys must be unique across the app — dynamic widget creation in loops requires explicit key= parameter to avoid DuplicateWidgetID errors

security_score_components.security_notes: Apps are publicly accessible by default — no auth built-in. Snowflake acquisition brings enterprise security options. Community Cloud apps can expose secrets if not using st.secrets properly. Open-source Apache 2.0 for auditability.
